---
title: "Live Migration Stack"
---

# Live Migration Stack

**Working seal:** `stack://live-migration-stack/23AA56B454`

**Enter:** Use when data, schemas, or infrastructure are changing in a live environment.


## Related Runes

- [0926 migration](../reference/house-databases-persistence-and-time-binding-words.qmd#rune-0926) - Stored reality changing shape without being lost. Good migrations are staged, inspectable, and reversible long enough to discover what the data actually contains.
- [0943 rollback {DB}](../reference/house-databases-persistence-and-time-binding-words.qmd#rune-0943) - Permission to retreat toward a previously trusted state. A system without rollback mistakes hope for safety.
- [0347 schema {Data}](../reference/house-data-state-and-representation.qmd#rune-0347) - The declared shape of stored meaning.
- [1461 idempotent](../reference/house-guarantee-words-quality-attributes-and-behavioral-adjectives.qmd#rune-1461) - The word that makes retries survivable: same request, same lasting effect.
- [1567 schema drift](../reference/house-failure-words-pathologies-and-counter-spells.qmd#rune-1567) - The stored world and the assumed world have silently stopped matching. This is how migrations keep hurting after everyone thinks they are done.


## Related Spell Templates and References

- [Migration Without Data Loss](../spells/migration-without-data-loss.qmd)
- [Stack Grammar](../reference/stack-grammar.qmd)
- [Failure Modes](../reference/failure-modes.qmd)
- [Canon Map](../reference/canon-map.qmd)
| Step | Spell             | Artifact                                  | Advance when                      |
| ---- | ----------------- | ----------------------------------------- | --------------------------------- |
| 1    | Inventory         | Dependency and blast-radius map           | Moving parts are known            |
| 2    | Prepare rollback  | Backup, snapshot, flag, or dual-read path | Reversal is real                  |
| 3    | Expand compatibly | Backward-compatible new structure         | Old paths still work              |
| 4    | Backfill          | New representation                        | Old path is not cut off           |
| 5    | Compare parity    | Parity report                             | Old and new paths agree           |
| 6    | Cut over          | Read or write switch                      | Parity holds and rollback is live |
| 7    | Observe           | Live metrics and divergence samples       | Safety window remains stable      |
| 8    | Contract          | Removed old paths                         | Safety window closes cleanly      |

## Loop or Recursion

```json
{
  "steps": [
    5,
    6,
    7
  ],
  "until": "Confidence is sufficient for cutover and contraction"
}
```


## On Fail

Rollback immediately using the prepared boundary and preserve divergence samples.

## Why It Works

It forces reversibility to exist before forward motion becomes expensive.
